threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of the Overground, and your face lights up at the sight of the 'unique' graffiti art near the station.
Caught a guy on the 333 rapping to himself while the bus swayed; bus driver seemed to nod along, whole vibe was surprisingly uplifting.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The smell of fresh currywurst mingling with the sound of late-night techno from Späti parties—only in Neukölln do your senses collide like that.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a park where locals turn up with grills and disco music, claiming the sunset as theirs while sipping on Berliner Weisse.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid the BART escalators during rush hour—always breaks down when the city's melting down with delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a full drum kit on the 38 Geary this morning. Only in San Francisco do you get a live show with your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always assume our bar terraces are for sunbathing. Most locals avoid them in summer; it’s too hot to enjoy a beer in the sun.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their enthusiasm for the 'best crepes' in Gràcia—it's usually just a tourist trap with long lines.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still take the bus to visit the West End, instead of hopping on the overground for a quicker route to Shoreditch.
Caught a night bus from Peckham last week, and a guy was rapping Shakespeare to the beat of a passing car. Only in South London, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw an elderly man feeding the pigeons in Hermannplatz, while a street musician played an accordion right next to him. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you'll spot food trucks serving gourmet döner like it's haute cuisine. It's the only place where kebab can have a Michelin star vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local if they instinctively know which BART station to avoid on a Friday evening after hearing about another brake overheating incident.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Witnessed a lady in a pink feather boa arguing with a bus driver over the scent of her perfume—only in the TL, folks.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it's all about Gaudí. But the real magic is in Gràcia's hidden plazas, where the locals sip vermut and share gossip, far from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro map, staring at it like it's a treasure map, while the locals glide past without a second glance.
